steps.title: Identify Grimes's stated goal
steps.content: Grimes wanted the poems in One Last Word to honor important Black poets of the past by using their lines through the golden shovel method.

steps.title: Map the reviewers' reaction to that goal
steps.content: The text concedes the method might seem to produce awkward poems, then pivots with "However" to say reviewers called the collection a beautiful and powerful tribute. Praising it as a successful tribute is direct evidence that her goal was met.

steps.title: Eliminate the wrong choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'most reviewers didn't understand Grimes's goal': contradicts the passage — reviewers explicitly recognized it as a tribute, which is the goal. ✗
  • Choice B — 'successfully used the golden shovel method to achieve her goal': positive reviews of a tribute = the method achieved its tribute purpose. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Hughes and Johnson are two of Grimes's favorites': overreach — the passage names them as poets she honored, not as personal favorites. ✗
  • Choice D — 'inspired many other writers to use the golden shovel method': nothing in the text says other writers were inspired; this goes beyond the evidence. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
